name: HEPATIC ANGIOSARCOMA
definition: HEMANGIOENDOTHELIAL SARCOMA = KUPFFER CELL SARCOMA = HEMANGIOSARCOMA

demographics.Prevalence: 0.14-0.25÷1,000,000; < 2% of all primary liver neoplasms; most common
demographics.Associated with: hemochromatosis, anabolic steroids, cirrhosis, von Recklinghausen disease
demographics.Histo: vascular derivation confirmed by reactivity to factor VIII-related antigen, CD31 and

dominant_finding: hypodense masses with high-density regions (hemorrhage) / low-attenuation regions (old
discriminating_features: hypodense masses with high-density regions (hemorrhage) / low-attenuation regions (old

NUC: single / multiple photopenic areas on sulfur colloid scan
perfusion blood pool mismatch (initial decrease followed by slow increase in RBC
US: solid / mixed mass with anechoic areas (hemorrhage / necrosis)
diffuse heterogeneous echotexture of entire liver
CT: hypodense masses with high-density regions (hemorrhage) / low-attenuation regions (old
focal areas of peripheral enhancement with fill-in on dynamic CT as in large hemangioma
MR: hypointense on T1WI with irregular areas of high signal (hemorrhage)
heterogeneously hyperintense on T2WI + fluid-fluid levels
markedly heterogeneous enhancement: predominantly peripheral, progressive on delayed
ANGIO: hypervascular stain around tumor periphery in late arterial phase with puddling; NO arterial
PET: may demonstrate marked uptake in liver tumor

clinical.Prognosis: rapid deterioration with median survival of 6 months (13 months under
